title: eleogenus ovatus
intro: Eleogenus ovatus, also known as the olive-leaved tree or sweet olive, is a plant species native to Australia that belongs to the Rutaceae family. It is a shrub or small tree with fragrant leaves and small white flowers, known for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Eleogenus ovatus include its use as an antiseptic, antimicrobial, and antifungal agent, as well as its potential to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The herbal preparations of Eleogenus ovatus include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using a solvent to extract its bioactive compounds.
